metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Instrumental Music 11 advances students' technical proficiency, musicianship, and ensemble skills, building on Instrumental Music 10. Students deepen their music literacy, explore increasingly complex and diverse repertoire, and develop independent musicianship and performance practice. Critical listening, score study, and artistic reflection are central to the course. First Peoples musical knowledge and diverse global traditions are engaged with respect. This course prepares students for Instrumental Music 12.
metadata.general_description: An advanced senior instrumental music course developing refined technical proficiency, independent musicianship, advanced music literacy, and ensemble leadership — preparing students for Instrumental Music 12 and post-secondary music pathways.

big_ideas.statement: Technical mastery enables musical expression and artistic freedom.
big_ideas.elaboration: As technical fluency grows, musicians gain greater capacity for expressive communication — moving beyond mechanical accuracy toward authentic musical interpretation and personal artistic voice.

big_ideas.statement: Music provides opportunities to gain deep insight into the perspectives and experiences of diverse cultures.
big_ideas.elaboration: Engaging with repertoire from a wide range of traditions — including Indigenous, world, and contemporary music — deepens cultural understanding and challenges assumptions about what music can be.

big_ideas.statement: Artistic growth requires sustained practice, critical self-reflection, and musical risk-taking.
big_ideas.elaboration: Advanced musicianship demands long-term commitment, honest self-assessment, openness to feedback, and the willingness to explore beyond one's comfort zone technically and expressively.

big_ideas.statement: Music is a cultural practice that transmits values, histories, and ways of knowing.
big_ideas.elaboration: Music is not just sound — it is a form of cultural knowledge. Every tradition carries with it a worldview, a history, and a set of values that the music both reflects and transmits.

competencies.raw: Demonstrate refined technical proficiency on instrument; apply musical elements with greater expressive range; explore diverse repertoire and musical styles; create through improvisation and arrangement.
i_can_statements: I can demonstrate refined technical proficiency — tone quality, intonation, agility, and range — on my instrument.
I can apply musical elements with growing expressive range: phrasing, dynamics, articulation, and stylistic nuance.
I can engage with diverse repertoire from multiple historical periods and cultural traditions.
I can create original musical ideas through improvisation, arrangement, and short composition tasks.

competencies.raw: Analyze musical works and performances using advanced vocabulary; apply feedback to refine musicianship; examine music in historical, cultural, and social context; engage in independent musical decision-making.
i_can_statements: I can analyze musical works using advanced terminology: form, harmonic structure, style period, and performance practice.
I can apply feedback to make specific, targeted improvements to my musical performance.
I can examine music in its historical, cultural, and social contexts, understanding how context shapes interpretation.
I can make independent musical decisions about phrasing, dynamics, and interpretation.

competencies.raw: Perform prepared and sight-read music for audiences with increasing independence; document musical growth; communicate musical understanding.
i_can_statements: I can perform prepared and sight-read music with increasing technical precision and musical independence.
I can document my musical growth through practice logs, recordings, and reflective analysis.
I can communicate musical insights about repertoire, interpretation, and performance practice.

competencies.raw: Demonstrate sustained professionalism and leadership in ensemble; engage with First Peoples musical traditions; connect music learning to broader community and cultural contexts.
i_can_statements: I can demonstrate sustained professionalism and emerging leadership in ensemble rehearsal and performance.
I can engage with First Peoples musical traditions respectfully, understanding their cultural significance and protocols.
I can connect music learning to community contexts — school, local, and cultural.

areas.name: Advanced Instrumental Technique
areas.raw: Advanced technique on a specific instrument.
topics: Advanced tone production: nuanced dynamics, tone colour, and articulation
Extended technique: col legno, flutter tongue, harmonics, multiphonics (instrument-dependent)
Advanced scale and arpeggio work: all major and minor keys, modes
Sight-reading at Grade 4-6 RCM/ABRSM or equivalent level
Solo repertoire study: learning and performing a substantial solo work

areas.name: Music Theory and Literacy
areas.raw: Advanced music literacy, theory, and analysis.
topics: Advanced rhythmic reading: complex subdivisions, mixed meter, and polyrhythm
Harmonic analysis: chord function, Roman numeral analysis, and modulation
Form analysis: binary, ternary, rondo, sonata form, and through-composed
Score study: analyzing conductor's scores and preparing personal parts
Transposition and concert vs. written pitch relationships

areas.name: Ensemble Performance
areas.raw: Advanced ensemble skills, repertoire, and concert performance.
topics: Section leadership: tuning, balance, and section coordination
Complex ensemble repertoire from multiple periods and traditions
Chamber music: small ensemble rehearsal and performance
Performance practice: historically informed performance styles
Concert programming and program notes

areas.name: Music History and Cultural Traditions
areas.raw: Diverse musical traditions, First Peoples music, and music history.
topics: Baroque through Contemporary Western music history: style characteristics and key composers
First Peoples musical traditions: contemporary Indigenous composers and musicians
World music in depth: select traditions — their cultural context, instruments, and forms
Jazz history and traditions: improvisation, blues form, and iconic performers
Music and identity: how music communities define cultural belonging
